channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gematsu.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gematsu.com

item.title: Clive Barker’s Hellraiser: Revival ‘Genesis Configuration’ trailer
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

A tool for both combat and puzzles.

Saber Interactive has released a new trailer for survival horror action game Clive Barker’s Hellraiser: Revival introducing the “Genesis Configuration,” a tool for both combat and puzzles.

Here are the latest details, via Saber Interactive:

The Genesis Configuration is calling to you. The puzzle box will not ask your desires… Only unravel the boundaries. True transformation requires suffering.

Your curiosity ignites, but flames that illuminate may also burn…

A mighty tool for both combat and puzzles in Clive Barker’s Hellraiser: Revival. Outside of combat, players can use the Genesis Configuration to transform parts of the Labyrinth to open paths forward. Manipulating the box will also be invaluable for solving puzzles in the world of mortals.

But the full power of the Genesis Configuration comes alive in combat. Banish your enemies with gruesome executions to gather their Suffering and unleash a variety of abilities with the Genesis Configuration. Use Telekinesis to launch saw blades, rail spikes and more at enemies to maim and dismember them. Wield Pyrokinesis to absorb incandescent fire to burn away foes and the environment. And summon forth the iconic Hell Chains to tear your opponents apart without mercy.

Clive Barker’s Hellraiser: Revival is due out for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam in 2026.

Watch the trailer below.

Genesis Configuration Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/05/clive-barkers-hellraiser-revival-genesis-configuration-trailer
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: State of Play set for June 2 featuring more than 60 minutes of announcements
item.content:encoded:
• PS5

Alamo Drafthouse live watch party also available in the United States.

Sony Interactive Entertainment will host a State of Play on June 2 at 2:00 p.m. PT / 5:00 p.m. ET, the company announced. You will be able to watch it on YouTube, Twitch, and TikTok.

The broadcast, which will run for more than 60 minutes, will feature updates, announcements, and gameplay reveals from “top studios around the world,” starting off with a closer look at Marvel’s Wolverine from Insomniac Games.

Sony Interactive Entertainment is also partnering with Alamo Drafthouse in the United States to host special live watch party viewings of the broadcast. Free, but limited ticket will be available at the Alamo Drafthouse website starting today at 6:00 a.m. PT / 9:00 a.m. ET. Available locations include Chicago, Dallas / Fort Worth, Los Angeles, New York City, Raleigh, and the San Francisco Bay Area.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/05/state-of-play-set-for-june-2-featuring-more-than-60-minutes-of-announcements
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Former Housemarque director Harry Krueger establishes game studio Cosmic Division
item.content:encoded:
First title to deliver "unapologetically gameplay-first experience."

Returnal and Nex Machina director Harry Krueger, who left Housemarque in 2023 after 14 years, has announced the establishment of COsmic Division, a new game development studio based in Helsinki, Finland.

“I‘m incredibly grateful to Housemarque for our shared journey, which has shaped the foundations for our new studio,” said Cosmic Division founder and CEO Harry Krueger in a press erlease. “We’re proud of what we’ve achieved, but we’re not standing still. At Cosmic Division, we’re building on our strengths while finding a new edge through fresh themes, ideas and talent. We believe there is always room for games built on timeless values that trust the player and leave a lasting impact.”

The studio will embrace a “focused and responsibly ambitious” approach to development, operating with a “lean-and-mean” philosophy that prioritizes “craftsmanship, fast iteration, and sustainable growth over scale.”

Cosmic Division’s first project will be a new intellectual property that “embodies Cosmic Division’s creative DNA,” designed as a single-player title for console and PC that delivers an “unapologetically gameplay-first experience and a story with strong emotional resonance.”

Krueger continued, “We’re excited to push further into the experiences we love creating: intoxicating gameplay loops, audiovisual spectacle, and impactful stories that linger with players long after the credits have rolled. We have our feet on the ground and have solid foundations beneath us. Now our journey of cosmic wonder begins, as we venture towards the stars… and beyond.”

An official announcement regarding the studio’s project will follow at a later date.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/05/former-housemarque-director-harry-krueger-establishes-game-studio-cosmic-division
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Warhorse Studios developing open-world Middle-earth RPG and new Kingdom Come adventure
item.content:encoded:
More details "when the time is right."

Kingdom Come: Deliverance series developer Warhorse Studios is developing an open-world RPG set in Middle-earth as well as “a new Kingdom Come adventure,” the studio announced.

Details about the projects were not announced.

“We’re excited to tell you more when the time is right,” Warhorse Studios said.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/05/warhorse-studios-developing-open-world-middle-earth-rpg-and-new-kingdom-come-adventure
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Console Archives Toujinmakyouden Hercules no Eikou launches May 21
item.content:encoded:
• PS5
  • Switch 2

Originally released by Data East in 1987.

Console Archives Toujinmakyouden Hercules no Eikou will launch for PlayStation 5 and Switch 2 on May 21 for $7.99 / 800 yen, developer Hamster announced.

Here is an overview of the game, via Hamster:

Rise up, Hero Hercules!

Toujinmakyouden Hercules ni Eikou was released by Data East in 1987 for an 8-bit home console.

This is a RPG game where you embark on a journey with the goal of defeating the Demon King Hades and rescuing the goddess Venus who has been taken to the underworld. Overcome the various difficulties that await, defeat the monsters that stand in your way, and gallantly rescue Venus!

(This title was originally released exclusively in Japan. While the user interface and manual are available in English, the game content is available in Japanese only.)

The Console Archives series is designed to bring classic games originally released on home consoles to modern gaming platforms.

In Console Archives series, features such as customizable button layouts, rapid-fire settings, multiple save points, and for some titles, a rewind function have been added, to support players in completing these classics.

Additional options include screen layout adjustments and filters that recreate the the nostalgic atmosphere of CRT TVs back in the day, allowing players to customize the visual experience to their preference.

Whether it’s a title you played long ago or one you’re experiencing for the first time, we hope you’ll find a favorite and enjoy the game your way.

Watch a trailer below.

Trailer

English

Japanese



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/05/console-archives-toujinmakyouden-hercules-no-eikou-launches-may-21
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Arcade Archives Tag Team Wrestling launches May 21
item.content:encoded:
• PS4
  • PS5
  • Switch
  • Switch 2
  • Xbox Series

Originally released by Technos Japan in 1983.

Arcade Archives 2 Tag Team Wrestling will launch for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and Switch 2, and Arcade Archives Tag Team Wrestling for PlayStation 4 and Switch, on May 21, developer Hamster announced. The Arcade Archives 2 version will cost $9.99 / 1,100 yen, while Arcade Archives version will cost $7.99 / 837 yen. An upgrade from Arcade Archives to Arcade Archives 2 will be available for $2.99 / 330 yen.

Here is an overview of the game, via Hamster:

The Big Excite Series finally begins!!

Tag Team Wrestling is an action game released by Technos Japan in 1983.

Enjoy the world of professional wrestling as you control the hero wrestler and aim to become world champion.

Quick move selection and the skillful tag team work unique to tag matches are the keys to victory!

Sometimes brawls spill out of the ring, and unexpected intruders might even show up!?

The Arcade Archives series is developed with the concept of faithfully reproducing classic arcade games, staying true to its original.

In Original Mode, you’ll find a variety of convenient features that make revisiting these classic titles even more enjoyable. These include difficulty and display settings, button remapping, rapid-fire options, the ability to create multiple save files at any time, and even a rewind function to undo your last move. Some titles also offer special settings that recreates the nostalgic atmosphere of CRT TVs back in the day. In addition, in Hi Score Mode and Caravan Mode, you can compete for the best scores with players around the world through Online Rankings.

Arcade Archives 2 series introduces a brand-new mode called Time Attack Mode, in addition to the Original Mode, Hi Score Mode, and Caravan Mode from Arcade Archives. Time Attack Mode focuses on how quickly you can complete the game, regardless of the score you achieve.

What’s more, with VRR (Variable Refresh Rate) support, the gameplay can be reproduced even more accurately, closer to the original arcade experience.

Please enjoy these iconic arcade games that defined an era with the “Arcade Archives” series!

Watch a trailer below.

Trailer

English

Japanese



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/05/arcade-archives-tag-team-wrestling-launches-may-21
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
